← all tutorials

Failure Detection: timeouts → phi accrual — interactive 📖 FAILURE_DETECTION.md 📄 failure_detection.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
A live node sends a heartbeat every ~1s; the monitor must bet on whether it's alive or dead. A fixed timeout cries wolf; phi accrual outputs a continuous suspicion level φ = −log10(P(inter-arrival > t)) that accrues the longer we wait. Drag the sliders — the φ numbers recompute live from the identical formula as failure_detection.py.  |  how Raft consumes this →

① Heartbeat timeline — drag the timeout, watch the false positives appear

What to do: lower the timeout threshold. With a fixed 3.0s the detector survives every gap — except the one 4.0s stall (the node is alive, the network is slow) where it false-positives. Push the threshold under ~1.1s and it screams on ordinary 1s jitter. There is no good fixed value.
replays STALL_TRACE from failure_detection.py Section A
heartbeat (alive) false positive (declared DEAD while alive) deadline = prev heartbeat + threshold

② Phi accrual — drag elapsed time, watch φ accrue; drag jitter, watch the curve reshape

What this shows: φ grows continuously the longer we wait. It crosses 1 (~10% FP) near 1.08s, 3 (~0.1%) near 1.20s, 8 (Cassandra's convict line) near 1.36s. Bump the jitter σ and the curve flattens — the same delay becomes less surprising (lower φ). Default σ = 0.0637 matches the WINDOW from failure_detection.py Section C.
μ = 1.000s (fixed). σ slider is exploration; the GOLD check uses σ=0.0637.
current suspicion level
φ = 3.08
green: alive
yellow: suspect
red: φ ≥ 8 → convict

suspicious

③ Comparison — simple vs adaptive vs phi on a heavy-tail network

What to do: click a detector row. All three watch the SAME live heavy-tail node (multi-second gaps are routine here, so they recur in the window). The fixed simple timeout cannot see that, so it false-positives 3×; adaptive & phi read the window and mostly stay quiet. Latency numbers recompute from the trace's final window.
check: … JS φ recompute matches failure_detection.py GOLD (μ=1.000000, σ=0.063660, φ(1.2)=3.075763) and the FP↔φ identity 10^(−φ).
